June 6 to 7, 2026 · Hiawatha National Forest

Iron & Pine Omnium

A two-day gravel series threading Alger and Marquette County with one goal: crown the strongest all-around rider in the forest.

Day one is a fondo-style group ride. Day two flips into a grinduro rhythm of timed sectors and KOM chases. Overall winners are calculated separately for men, women, and non-binary riders.

See the Omnium Leaderboard How the weekend works
Forest roads
Sector speed
KOM points

The omnium equation

Day 1 Moving time
Day 2 Sectors + KOM
Categories Men · Women · Non-Binary

Weekend Format

Two linked rides, one overall standing.

Companion site

Day 1 · MK Ultra Gravel

An all-day fondo-style group ride where moving time still matters, even though the opening day is not a formal race.

Visit event website

Companion site

Day 2 · Hiawatha's Revenge

A grinduro-style day across Alger and Marquette County where gravel sectors and climb points decide the weekend.

Visit event website

Scoring System

Fastest benchmarks are normalized to 100, then the weekend gets weighted.

35% weighted contribution

Day 1 moving time

Each rider is benchmarked against the fastest moving time in their category. The result is weighted so the fondo counts without turning day one into a pure race.

45% weighted contribution

Day 2 gravel sectors

All timed gravel sectors are totaled, benchmarked against the fastest category aggregate, and weighted most heavily to reflect the grinduro focus.

20% weighted contribution

Day 2 KOM points

Climb points give aggressive riders a way to move up the standings even if they are not the fastest rider across the full sector set.

Formula

Overall score = day 1 benchmark ratio × 35 + day 2 sector benchmark ratio × 45 + KOM benchmark ratio × 20

Each category is ranked independently. The leaderboard below uses sample rider data so the model and UI can be validated before importing real results.

Leaderboard

Three categories. Three score inputs. One omnium winner in each field.

Live Omnium Model

Overall winners are calculated inside each category.
